name: Sohrab
description: Sohrab is a tragic hero from Persian mythology, most famously known from Ferdowsi's epic poem, the Shahnameh (Book of Kings). He is the son of Rostam, the greatest warrior in Persian legend, and Tahmineh, the princess of Samangan. Unbeknownst to Rostam, Tahmineh conceals her pregnancy and raises Sohrab in secret, fearing that Rostam's enemies would harm the child. Sohrab grows into a powerful and skilled warrior, rivaling even his father in strength and prowess. Driven by a desire to find his father and prove his worth, Sohrab sets out to conquer Iran, believing that Rostam will join him once he sees his son's might. He leads the Turanian army against the Iranian forces, engaging in numerous battles and demonstrating his unmatched martial abilities. The tragic climax of Sohrab's story arrives when he faces Rostam in single combat. Neither man knows the other's true identity, and their battle is fierce and prolonged. Rostam, using a trick he learned from his father, mortally wounds Sohrab. As Sohrab lies dying, he reveals his identity, and Rostam is overcome with grief and remorse at having killed his own son. Sohrab's story is a poignant tale of missed connections, fate, and the devastating consequences of war and ignorance. He represents youthful strength, unrealized potential, and the tragedy of familial conflict.
subtitle: Tragic hero of the Shahnameh
achievements: Mastered combat skills at a young age
Led the Turanian army against Iran
Proved his strength and valor in battle

keyMyths.title: Birth and Secret Upbringing
keyMyths.description: Sohrab was born in secret to Rostam and Tahmineh. Tahmineh hid his existence from Rostam, raising him in Samangan and concealing his true parentage to protect him.

keyMyths.title: Sohrab's Quest
keyMyths.description: Driven by ambition and a desire to find his father, Sohrab sets out to conquer Iran. He believes that Rostam will recognize him and join his cause once he sees his power.

keyMyths.title: The Fatal Duel
keyMyths.description: Sohrab and Rostam engage in a fierce duel, unaware of their familial connection. Rostam ultimately defeats and kills Sohrab, only to discover his identity after it is too late.

sources.author: Ferdowsi
sources.description: The primary source for the story of Sohrab and Rostam.
sources.text: The primary source for the story of Sohrab and Rostam.
